design_philosophy.core_loop: Select a piece of your color and move it 1-2 squares away; all adjacent pieces flip to your color. Repeats turn-by-turn with 2-4 players.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Territory control and piece majority on a shared 7x7 board; turn-based spatial positioning strategy.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Engaging in abstract strategy with immediate visual gratification—Spot character animates each move with contextual actions (dance, rollerskate, cartwheel, dive, fall backward).
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Transforms Othello/Reversi (1883 board game) into a 7Up brand tie-in with minimal changes to core rules but emphasizes multiplayer (up to 4 players) and character-driven animation as the novelty rather than mechanical innovation. Designed as a budget title elevated to full-price via licensing.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Scalable CPU opponent difficulty levels (CPU level 5 confirmed as highest); players can face single or multiple AI opponents or engage in hot-seat multiplayer with human competition.

replayability_hooks: Adjustable board configurations with unavailable tile locations
Scalable CPU difficulty levels for single-player variety
Animated character sequences vary based on movement direction and proximity (1 vs 2 squares)

world_building_techniques: 7Up mascot branding and character animations provide thematic wrapper around abstract strategy mechanics

faction_indicators: Four distinct player colors (each designated to a specific player in multiplayer mode)
danger_signals: Piece majority loss on board—opponent territory control indicating strategic disadvantage
environmental_storytelling: 7x7 board state serves as visual representation of player strength relative to opponents; board layout variation (with unavailable tiles) creates distinct play scenarios

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Spot: The Video Game (NES)
